# GRAPH.CONFIG-GET | FalkorDB Docs

[Skip to main content](https://docs.falkordb.com/commands/graph.config-get.html#main-content)
 Link Menu Expand (external link) Document Search Copy Copied

[](https://www.falkordb.com/)

 Search FalkorDB Docs

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/commands/graph.config-get.html#graphconfig-get)
GRAPH.CONFIG-GET
=============================================================================================

Retrieves the current value of a FalkorDB configuration parameter.

FalkorDB configuration parameters are detailed [here](https://docs.falkordb.com/configuration)
.

`*` can be used to retrieve the value of all FalkorDB configuration parameters.

 python  javascript  rust  java  shell

Copy

    
    from falkordb import FalkorDB
    client = FalkorDB()
    config = client.get_config('*')
    print(config)
    

Copy

    
    import { FalkorDB } from 'falkordb';
    const client = await FalkorDB.connect();
    const config = await client.getConfig('*');
    console.log(config);
    

Copy

    
    let client = FalkorDB::connect_default();
    let config = client.get_config("*")?;
    println!("{:?}", config);
    

Copy

    
    FalkorDB client = new FalkorDB();
    Map<String, Object> config = client.getConfig("*");
    System.out.println(config);
    

Copy

    
    graph.config get *
    # Output:
    # 1) 1) "TIMEOUT"
    #    2) (integer) 0
    # ...
    

 python  javascript  rust  java  shell

Copy

    
    timeout = client.get_config('TIMEOUT')
    print(timeout)
    

Copy

    
    const timeout = await client.getConfig('TIMEOUT');
    console.log(timeout);
    

Copy

    
    let timeout = client.get_config("TIMEOUT")?;
    println!("{:?}", timeout);
    

Copy

    
    Object timeout = client.getConfig("TIMEOUT");
    System.out.println(timeout);
    

Copy

    
    graph.config get TIMEOUT
    # Output:
    # 1) "TIMEOUT"
    # 2) (integer) 0
    

* * *

---

# GRAPH.CONSTRAINT DROP | FalkorDB Docs

[Skip to main content](https://docs.falkordb.com/commands/graph.constraint-drop.html#main-content)
 Link Menu Expand (external link) Document Search Copy Copied

[](https://www.falkordb.com/)

 Search FalkorDB Docs

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/commands/graph.constraint-drop.html#graphconstraint-drop)
GRAPH.CONSTRAINT DROP
============================================================================================================

* * *

syntax: | GRAPH.CONSTRAINT DROP key MANDATORY|UNIQUE NODE label | RELATIONSHIP reltype PROPERTIES propCount prop \[prop…\]  
—

Deleted a graph constraint.

[Examples](https://docs.falkordb.com/commands/graph.constraint-drop.html#examples)

For an introduction to constraints see [GRAPH.CONSTRAINT CREATE](https://docs.falkordb.com/commands/graph.constraint-create)

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/commands/graph.constraint-drop.html#required-arguments)
Required arguments
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`key` is key name for the graph. `constraintType` is the constraint type: either \`MANDATORY\` or \`UNIQUE\`. `NODE label | RELATIONSHIP reltype` is the graph entity type (\`NODE\` or \`RELATIONSHIP\`) and the name of the node label or relationship type on which the constraint is enforced. `propCount` is the number of properties following. Valid values are between 1 and 255. `prop...` is a list of \`propCount\` property names.

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/commands/graph.constraint-drop.html#return-value)
Return value
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

@simple-string-reply - `OK` if executed correctly, or @error-reply otherwise.

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/commands/graph.constraint-drop.html#examples)
Examples
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To delete a unique constraint for all nodes with label `Person` enforcing uniqueness on the combination of values of attributes `first_name` and `last_name`, issue the following command:

 python  javascript  rust  java  shell

Copy

    
    from falkordb import FalkorDB
    client = FalkorDB()
    result = client.drop_constraint('g', 'UNIQUE', 'NODE', 'Person', ['first_name', 'last_name'])
    print(result)
    

Copy

    
    import { FalkorDB } from 'falkordb';
    const client = await FalkorDB.connect();
    const result = await client.dropConstraint('g', 'UNIQUE', 'NODE', 'Person', ['first_name', 'last_name']);
    console.log(result);
    

Copy

    
    let client = FalkorDB::connect_default();
    let result = client.drop_constraint("g", "UNIQUE", "NODE", "Person", &["first_name", "last_name"])?;
    println!("{}", result);
    

Copy

    
    FalkorDB client = new FalkorDB();
    String result = client.dropConstraint("g", "UNIQUE", "NODE", "Person", Arrays.asList("first_name", "last_name"));
    System.out.println(result);
    

Copy

    
    redis> GRAPH.CONSTRAINT DROP g UNIQUE NODE Person PROPERTIES 2 first_name last_name
    # Output: OK
    

* * *

---

# text.indexOf | FalkorDB Docs

[Skip to main content](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/indexOf.html#main-content)
 Link Menu Expand (external link) Document Search Copy Copied

[](https://www.falkordb.com/)

 Search FalkorDB Docs

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/indexOf.html#textindexof)
text.indexOf
==================================================================================

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/indexOf.html#description)
Description
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Finds the first occurrence of a substring within a string, optionally starting from a specific offset and ending at a specific position.

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/indexOf.html#syntax)
Syntax
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

    flex.text.indexOf(string, substring, offset, to)
    

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/indexOf.html#parameters)
Parameters
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `string` | string | Yes | The string to search in |
| `substring` | string | Yes | The substring to search for |
| `offset` | number | No  | Starting position for search (default: 0) |
| `to` | number | No  | Ending position for search (default: -1, meaning end of string) |

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/indexOf.html#returns)
Returns
-------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Type:** number (integer)

The zero-based index of the first occurrence of the substring, or `-1` if not found. Returns `null` if the input string is `null`.

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/indexOf.html#examples)
Examples
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

### [](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/indexOf.html#example-1-basic-search)
Example 1: Basic Search

    RETURN flex.text.indexOf('hello world', 'world') AS position
    

**Output:**

    position
    --------
    6
    

### [](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/indexOf.html#example-2-search-with-offset)
Example 2: Search with Offset

    RETURN flex.text.indexOf('hello hello', 'hello', 3) AS position
    

**Output:**

    position
    --------
    6
    

(Finds the second “hello” starting from position 3)

### [](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/indexOf.html#example-3-filtering-nodes-by-substring-position)
Example 3: Filtering Nodes by Substring Position

    MATCH (p:Product)
    WHERE flex.text.indexOf(p.description, 'premium') >= 0
    RETURN p.name, p.description
    

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/indexOf.html#notes)
Notes
---------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Returns `null` if input string is `null`
*   Returns `-1` if substring is not found
*   Uses zero-based indexing
*   The `offset` parameter allows starting search from a specific position
*   The `to` parameter limits search to a specific range

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/indexOf.html#see-also)
See Also
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [text.indexesOf](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/indexesOf.html)
     - Find all occurrences of a substring
*   [text.replace](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/replace.html)
     - Replace substring occurrences

* * *

---

# sim.jaccard | FalkorDB Docs

[Skip to main content](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/similarity/jaccard.html#main-content)
 Link Menu Expand (external link) Document Search Copy Copied

[](https://www.falkordb.com/)

 Search FalkorDB Docs

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/similarity/jaccard.html#simjaccard)
sim.jaccard
======================================================================================

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/similarity/jaccard.html#description)
Description
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Computes the Jaccard similarity coefficient between two sets (lists). The Jaccard index measures the similarity between two sets by dividing the size of their intersection by the size of their union. It returns a value between 0 (no similarity) and 1 (identical sets).

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/similarity/jaccard.html#syntax)
Syntax
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

    flex.sim.jaccard(list1, list2)
    

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/similarity/jaccard.html#parameters)
Parameters
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `list1` | list | Yes | The first list to compare |
| `list2` | list | Yes | The second list to compare |

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/similarity/jaccard.html#returns)
Returns
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Type:** number (float)

A value between 0 and 1 representing the Jaccard similarity coefficient:

*   `1.0` indicates identical sets
*   `0.0` indicates no common elements
*   Returns `null` for invalid inputs

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/similarity/jaccard.html#examples)
Examples
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### [](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/similarity/jaccard.html#example-1-basic-set-similarity)
Example 1: Basic Set Similarity

    // Compare two tag lists
    RETURN flex.sim.jaccard(['tag1', 'tag2', 'tag3'], ['tag2', 'tag3', 'tag4']) AS similarity
    

**Output:**

    similarity
    ----------
    0.5
    

(2 common elements / 4 total unique elements = 0.5)

### [](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/similarity/jaccard.html#example-2-finding-similar-documents-by-tags)
Example 2: Finding Similar Documents by Tags

    // Find documents with similar tags to a reference document
    MATCH (ref:Document {id: 'doc123'})
    MATCH (other:Document)
    WHERE other.id <> ref.id
    WITH ref, other, flex.sim.jaccard(ref.tags, other.tags) AS similarity
    WHERE similarity > 0.3
    RETURN other.title, similarity
    ORDER BY similarity DESC
    LIMIT 10
    

### [](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/similarity/jaccard.html#example-3-user-interest-matching)
Example 3: User Interest Matching

    // Find users with similar interests
    MATCH (u1:User {id: $userId})
    MATCH (u2:User)
    WHERE u1 <> u2
    WITH u1, u2, flex.sim.jaccard(u1.interests, u2.interests) AS match_score
    WHERE match_score > 0.5
    RETURN u2.name, u2.interests, match_score
    ORDER BY match_score DESC
    

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/similarity/jaccard.html#notes)
Notes
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Treats input lists as sets (duplicates within each list don’t affect the result)
*   Returns `null` if either input is not an array
*   Order of elements doesn’t matter
*   Works with any comparable data types (strings, numbers, etc.)
*   Ideal for comparing categorical attributes, tags, or interest lists

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/similarity/jaccard.html#see-also)
See Also
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [text.levenshtein](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/levenshtein.html)
     - Edit distance for string comparison
*   [coll.intersection](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/collections/intersection.html)
     - Get common elements between sets
*   [coll.union](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/collections/union.html)
     - Combine sets

* * *

---

# text.indexesOf | FalkorDB Docs

[Skip to main content](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/indexesOf.html#main-content)
 Link Menu Expand (external link) Document Search Copy Copied

[](https://www.falkordb.com/)

 Search FalkorDB Docs

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/indexesOf.html#textindexesof)
text.indexesOf
========================================================================================

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/indexesOf.html#description)
Description
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Finds all occurrences of a substring within a string, returning an array of all matching positions. Optionally search within a specific range.

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/indexesOf.html#syntax)
Syntax
-------------------------------------------------------------------------

    flex.text.indexesOf(string, substring, from, to)
    

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/indexesOf.html#parameters)
Parameters
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `string` | string | Yes | The string to search in |
| `substring` | string | Yes | The substring to search for |
| `from` | number | No  | Starting position for search (default: 0) |
| `to` | number | No  | Ending position for search (default: -1, meaning end of string) |

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/indexesOf.html#returns)
Returns
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Type:** list of numbers

An array containing the zero-based indices of all occurrences of the substring. Returns an empty array if no matches are found. Returns `null` if the input string is `null`.

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/indexesOf.html#examples)
Examples
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

### [](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/indexesOf.html#example-1-find-all-occurrences)
Example 1: Find All Occurrences

    RETURN flex.text.indexesOf('hello hello hello', 'hello') AS positions
    

**Output:**

    positions
    -----------
    [0, 6, 12]
    

### [](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/indexesOf.html#example-2-find-occurrences-in-range)
Example 2: Find Occurrences in Range

    RETURN flex.text.indexesOf('abcabcabc', 'abc', 1, 9) AS positions
    

**Output:**

    positions
    ---------
    [3, 6]
    

(Skips first ‘abc’ at position 0, finds those within range)

### [](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/indexesOf.html#example-3-count-keyword-occurrences)
Example 3: Count Keyword Occurrences

    MATCH (d:Document)
    WITH d, flex.text.indexesOf(d.content, 'important') AS occurrences
    WHERE size(occurrences) > 2
    RETURN d.title, size(occurrences) AS importanceScore
    ORDER BY importanceScore DESC
    

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/indexesOf.html#notes)
Notes
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Returns `null` if input string is `null`
*   Returns empty array if substring is not found
*   Uses zero-based indexing
*   The `from` parameter allows starting search from a specific position
*   The `to` parameter limits search to a specific range
*   Useful for counting occurrences or analyzing text patterns

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/indexesOf.html#see-also)
See Also
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [text.indexOf](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/indexOf.html)
     - Find first occurrence only
*   [text.replace](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/replace.html)
     - Replace substring occurrences
*   [text.regexGroups](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/regexGroups.html)
     - Find matches using regex patterns

* * *

---

# coll.intersection | FalkorDB Docs

[Skip to main content](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/collections/intersection.html#main-content)
 Link Menu Expand (external link) Document Search Copy Copied

[](https://www.falkordb.com/)

 Search FalkorDB Docs

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/collections/intersection.html#collintersection)
coll.intersection
========================================================================================================

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/collections/intersection.html#description)
Description
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Finds the common elements between two lists, returning a new list containing only the elements that appear in both input lists.

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/collections/intersection.html#syntax)
Syntax
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

    flex.coll.intersection(list1, list2)
    

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/collections/intersection.html#parameters)
Parameters
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `list1` | list | Yes | The first list |
| `list2` | list | Yes | The second list |

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/collections/intersection.html#returns)
Returns
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Type:** list

A new list containing only the elements that exist in both input lists. Preserves the order from the first list.

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/collections/intersection.html#examples)
Examples
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### [](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/collections/intersection.html#example-1-basic-intersection)
Example 1: Basic Intersection

    WITH [1, 2, 3, 4] AS a, [3, 4, 5, 6] AS b
    RETURN flex.coll.intersection(a, b) AS result
    

**Output:**

    result
    -------
    [3, 4]
    

### [](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/collections/intersection.html#example-2-finding-common-tags)
Example 2: Finding Common Tags

    MATCH (d1:Document {id: 'doc1'})
    MATCH (d2:Document {id: 'doc2'})
    WITH flex.coll.intersection(d1.tags, d2.tags) AS commonTags
    WHERE size(commonTags) > 0
    RETURN commonTags
    

### [](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/collections/intersection.html#example-3-finding-users-with-shared-interests)
Example 3: Finding Users with Shared Interests

    MATCH (u1:User {id: $userId1})
    MATCH (u2:User {id: $userId2})
    WITH flex.coll.intersection(u1.interests, u2.interests) AS sharedInterests
    RETURN u1.name, u2.name, sharedInterests, size(sharedInterests) AS commonCount
    

### [](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/collections/intersection.html#example-4-filter-by-allowed-values)
Example 4: Filter by Allowed Values

    WITH ['admin', 'read', 'write', 'delete'] AS allowed
    MATCH (u:User)
    WITH u, flex.coll.intersection(u.permissions, allowed) AS validPerms
    RETURN u.name, validPerms
    

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/collections/intersection.html#notes)
Notes
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Returns elements that exist in both lists
*   Preserves the order from the first list
*   If an element appears multiple times in list1, each occurrence is checked against list2
*   Efficient implementation using Set for fast lookup
*   Equivalent to mathematical set intersection operation

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/collections/intersection.html#see-also)
See Also
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [coll.union](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/collections/union.html)
     - Combine all unique elements from both lists
*   [sim.jaccard](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/similarity/jaccard.html)
     - Calculate similarity coefficient using intersection

* * *

---

# text.jaroWinkler | FalkorDB Docs

[Skip to main content](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/jaroWinkler.html#main-content)
 Link Menu Expand (external link) Document Search Copy Copied

[](https://www.falkordb.com/)

 Search FalkorDB Docs

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/jaroWinkler.html#textjarowinkler)
text.jaroWinkler
==============================================================================================

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/jaroWinkler.html#description)
Description
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Computes the Jaro-Winkler similarity between two strings. This metric is particularly effective for short strings like names and addresses. It gives more favorable ratings to strings that match from the beginning. Returns a value between 0 (no similarity) and 1 (exact match).

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/jaroWinkler.html#syntax)
Syntax
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

    flex.text.jaroWinkler(string1, string2)
    

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/jaroWinkler.html#parameters)
Parameters
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `string1` | string | Yes | The first string to compare |
| `string2` | string | Yes | The second string to compare |

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/jaroWinkler.html#returns)
Returns
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Type:** number (float)

A similarity score between 0 and 1:

*   `1.0` indicates an exact match
*   `0.0` indicates no similarity
*   Higher values indicate greater similarity

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/jaroWinkler.html#examples)
Examples
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### [](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/jaroWinkler.html#example-1-name-matching)
Example 1: Name Matching

    // Compare similar names
    RETURN flex.text.jaroWinkler('Martha', 'Marhta') AS similarity
    

**Output:**

    similarity
    ----------
    0.961
    

### [](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/jaroWinkler.html#example-2-fuzzy-name-search)
Example 2: Fuzzy Name Search

    // Find people with names similar to "William"
    MATCH (p:Person)
    WHERE flex.text.jaroWinkler(p.firstName, 'William') > 0.85
    RETURN p.firstName, p.lastName, flex.text.jaroWinkler(p.firstName, 'William') AS score
    ORDER BY score DESC
    

### [](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/jaroWinkler.html#example-3-deduplication-by-company-name)
Example 3: Deduplication by Company Name

    // Find potential duplicate company records
    MATCH (c1:Company)
    MATCH (c2:Company)
    WHERE id(c1) < id(c2)
    WITH c1, c2, flex.text.jaroWinkler(c1.name, c2.name) AS similarity
    WHERE similarity > 0.9
    RETURN c1.name, c2.name, similarity
    ORDER BY similarity DESC
    

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/jaroWinkler.html#notes)
Notes
-------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Particularly effective for short strings (names, addresses)
*   Gives higher weight to strings that match from the beginning
*   Handles `null` values by returning appropriate default values
*   Case-sensitive comparison
*   More forgiving than exact match but stricter than pure Jaro similarity
*   Commonly used in record linkage and deduplication tasks

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/jaroWinkler.html#see-also)
See Also
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [text.levenshtein](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/levenshtein.html)
     - Edit distance metric for string comparison
*   [sim.jaccard](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/similarity/jaccard.html)
     - Set-based similarity

* * *

---

# Apache Jena | FalkorDB Docs

[Skip to main content](https://docs.falkordb.com/integration/jena.html#main-content)
 Link Menu Expand (external link) Document Search Copy Copied

[](https://www.falkordb.com/)

 Search FalkorDB Docs

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/integration/jena.html#apache-jena-integration)
Apache Jena Integration
===================================================================================================

This page describes how to integrate FalkorDB with [Apache Jena](https://jena.apache.org/)
 using the [jena-falkordb-adapter](https://github.com/FalkorDB/jena-falkordb-adapter)
.

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/integration/jena.html#overview)
Overview
---------------------------------------------------------------------

The jena-falkordb-adapter allows applications built on Jena to use FalkorDB as a backend for RDF data storage and querying. This enables seamless graph data management and SPARQL query execution on FalkorDB.

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/integration/jena.html#getting-started)
Getting Started
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Follow the [GETTING\_STARTED.md](https://github.com/FalkorDB/jena-falkordb-adapter/blob/main/GETTING_STARTED.md)
 guide for step-by-step instructions on setting up the adapter and connecting Jena to FalkorDB.

### [](https://docs.falkordb.com/integration/jena.html#install-from-maven)
Install from Maven

Add the FalkorDB Jena adapter dependency to your project’s `pom.xml`. Replace the version with the latest release from the adapter repository.

    <dependency>
      <groupId>com.falkordb</groupId>
      <artifactId>jena-falkordb-adapter</artifactId>
      <version>0.2.0</version>
    </dependency>
    

If the adapter is published to a non-standard Maven repository, add the repository block shown in the adapter’s README. Otherwise the dependency should resolve from Maven Central (if published there).

### [](https://docs.falkordb.com/integration/jena.html#quick-getting-started-java)
Quick Getting Started (Java)

The snippet below shows a minimal Jena program that registers the FalkorDB adapter and runs a simple SPARQL query. This is intentionally small — see the adapter’s `GETTING_STARTED.md` and `Main.java` for a complete example with configuration and connection options.

    import org.apache.jena.query.*;
    import org.apache.jena.rdf.model.Model;
    
    public class JenaFalkorExample {
        public static void main(String[] args) {
            // Create or obtain a Jena Model backed by FalkorDB.
            // The exact factory method depends on the adapter; check the adapter README/Main.java for the
            // connection options (hostname, port, credentials, etc.).
            Model model = FalkorDBJenaFactory.createModel("http://localhost:7474");
    
            // Example: add a triple
            model.createResource("http://example.org/alice")
                 .addProperty(model.createProperty("http://example.org/knows"),
                              model.createResource("http://example.org/bob"));
    
            // Run a simple SPARQL SELECT
            String sparql = "SELECT ?s ?p ?o WHERE { ?s ?p ?o } LIMIT 10";
            try (QueryExecution qexec = QueryExecutionFactory.create(sparql, model)) {
                ResultSet rs = qexec.execSelect();
                ResultSetFormatter.out(System.out, rs);
            }
    
            // Remember to close the model when finished
            model.close();
        }
    }
    

Notes:

*   Replace `FalkorDBJenaFactory.createModel(...)` with the actual adapter factory call used in the adapter (see `Main.java`).
*   Use the adapter’s configuration options (connection URL, authentication) when creating the model.

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/integration/jena.html#usage-example)
Usage Example
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

See the [Main.java](https://github.com/FalkorDB/jena-falkordb-adapter/blob/main/src/main/java/com/falkordb/jena/Main.java)
 for a sample Java application demonstrating how to use the adapter.

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/integration/jena.html#reference)
Reference
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [Adapter README](https://github.com/FalkorDB/jena-falkordb-adapter/blob/main/README.md)
    
*   [Getting Started](https://github.com/FalkorDB/jena-falkordb-adapter/blob/main/GETTING_STARTED.md)
    
*   [Sample Main.java](https://github.com/FalkorDB/jena-falkordb-adapter/blob/main/src/main/java/com/falkordb/jena/Main.java)
    

* * *

---

# Known limitations | FalkorDB Docs

[Skip to main content](https://docs.falkordb.com/cypher/known-limitations.html#main-content)
 Link Menu Expand (external link) Document Search Copy Copied

[](https://www.falkordb.com/)

 Search FalkorDB Docs

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/cypher/known-limitations.html#known-limitations)
Known limitations
===============================================================================================

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/cypher/known-limitations.html#relationship-uniqueness-in-patterns)
Relationship uniqueness in patterns
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When a relation in a match pattern is not referenced elsewhere in the query, FalkorDB will only verify that at least one matching relation exists (rather than operating on every matching relation).

In some queries, this will cause unexpected behaviors. Consider a graph with 2 nodes and 2 relations between them:

    CREATE (a)-[:e {val: '1'}]->(b), (a)-[:e {val: '2'}]->(b)
    

Counting the number of explicit edges returns 2, as expected.

    MATCH (a)-[e]->(b) RETURN COUNT(e)
    

However, if we count the nodes in this pattern without explicitly referencing the relation, we receive a value of 1.

    MATCH (a)-[e]->(b) RETURN COUNT(b)
    

As a temporary workaround, queries that must operate on every relation matching a pattern should explicitly refer to that relation’s alias elsewhere in the query. Two options for this are:

    MATCH (a)-[e]->(b) WHERE ID(e) >= 0 RETURN COUNT(b)
    MATCH (a)-[e]->(b) RETURN COUNT(b), e.dummyval
    

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/cypher/known-limitations.html#limit-clause-does-not-affect-eager-operations)
LIMIT clause does not affect eager operations
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When a WITH or RETURN clause introduces a LIMIT value, this value ought to be respected by all preceding operations.

For example, given the query:

    UNWIND [1,2,3] AS value CREATE (a {property: value}) RETURN a LIMIT 1
    

One node should be created with its ‘property’ set to 1. FalkorDB will currently create three nodes, and only return the first.

This limitation affects all eager operations: CREATE, SET, DELETE, MERGE, and projections with aggregate functions.

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/cypher/known-limitations.html#indexing-limitations)
Indexing limitations
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

One way in which FalkorDB will optimize queries is by introducing index scans when a filter is specified on an indexed label-property pair.

The current index implementation, however, does not handle not-equal (`<>`) filters.

To profile a query and see whether index optimizations have been introduced, use the `GRAPH.EXPLAIN` endpoint:

    $ redis-cli GRAPH.EXPLAIN social "MATCH (p:person) WHERE p.id < 5 RETURN p"
    1) "Results"
    2) "    Project"
    3) "        Index Scan | (p:person)"
    

* * *

---

# FLEX Function Reference | FalkorDB Docs

[Skip to main content](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/#main-content)
 Link Menu Expand (external link) Document Search Copy Copied

[](https://www.falkordb.com/)

 Search FalkorDB Docs

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/#flex-function-reference)
FLEX Function Reference
========================================================================================

FLEX is FalkorDB’s open source community UDF package, available at [github.com/FalkorDB/flex](https://github.com/FalkorDB/flex)
.  
It contains a variety of useful functionality, including:

*   String and set similarity metrics for fuzzy matching and comparison
*   Date and time manipulation, formatting, and parsing
*   Low-level bitwise operations on integers

We welcome contributions to extend this library with additional functionality.

The following sections document all FLEX (FalkorDB Library for Extensions) functions.

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/#function-categories)
Function Categories
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### [](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/#similarity-functions-flexsim)
Similarity Functions (`flex.sim.*`)

Set similarity metrics for fuzzy matching and comparison.

| Function | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [sim.jaccard](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/similarity/jaccard.html) | Calculate Jaccard similarity coefficient between sets |

### [](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/#text-functions-flextext)
Text Functions (`flex.text.*`)

String manipulation, formatting, case conversion utilities, and string similarity metrics.

| Function | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [text.capitalize](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/capitalize.html) | Capitalize the first character of a string |
| [text.decapitalize](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/decapitalize.html) | Lowercase the first character of a string |
| [text.swapCase](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/swapCase.html) | Swap the case of all characters in a string |
| [text.camelCase](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/camelCase.html) | Convert string to camelCase format |
| [text.upperCamelCase](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/upperCamelCase.html) | Convert string to UpperCamelCase (PascalCase) |
| [text.snakeCase](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/snakeCase.html) | Convert string to snake\_case format |
| [text.format](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/format.html) | Format string with placeholder substitution |
| [text.indexOf](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/indexOf.html) | Find first occurrence of substring |
| [text.indexesOf](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/indexesOf.html) | Find all occurrences of substring |
| [text.join](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/join.html) | Join array elements with delimiter |
| [text.lpad](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/lpad.html) | Pad string on the left to target length |
| [text.rpad](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/rpad.html) | Pad string on the right to target length |
| [text.regexGroups](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/regexGroups.html) | Extract regex matches and capture groups |
| [text.repeat](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/repeat.html) | Repeat string multiple times |
| [text.replace](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/replace.html) | Replace text using regex pattern |
| [text.jaroWinkler](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/jaroWinkler.html) | Compute Jaro-Winkler similarity for short strings |
| [text.levenshtein](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/levenshtein.html) | Compute Levenshtein edit distance between strings |

### [](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/#collection-functions-flexcoll)
Collection Functions (`flex.coll.*`)

Operations on lists and arrays including set operations and transformations.

| Function | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [coll.zip](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/collections/zip.html) | Combine two lists element-by-element into pairs |
| [coll.union](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/collections/union.html) | Combine lists and return unique elements |
| [coll.intersection](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/collections/intersection.html) | Find common elements between lists |
| [coll.shuffle](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/collections/shuffle.html) | Randomly shuffle list elements |
| [coll.frequencies](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/collections/frequencies.html) | Count frequency of each element in list |

### [](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/#map-functions-flexmap)
Map Functions (`flex.map.*`)

Map/object manipulation for property management and transformation.

| Function | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [map.merge](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/map/merge.html) | Shallow merge multiple maps |
| [map.fromPairs](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/map/fromPairs.html) | Convert list of key-value pairs to map |
| [map.submap](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/map/submap.html) | Extract subset of keys from map |
| [map.removeKey](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/map/removeKey.html) | Remove single key from map |
| [map.removeKeys](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/map/removeKeys.html) | Remove multiple keys from map |

### [](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/#json-functions-flexjson)
JSON Functions (`flex.json.*`)

JSON serialization and parsing utilities.

| Function | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [json.toJson](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/json/toJson.html) | Serialize value to JSON string |
| [json.fromJsonMap](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/json/fromJsonMap.html) | Parse JSON string to map |
| [json.fromJsonList](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/json/fromJsonList.html) | Parse JSON string to list |

### [](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/#date-functions-flexdate)
Date Functions (`flex.date.*`)

Date and time manipulation, formatting, and parsing.

| Function | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [date.format](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/date/format.html) | Format date/time with pattern and timezone |
| [date.parse](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/date/parse.html) | Parse date/time string with optional pattern |
| [date.truncate](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/date/truncate.html) | Truncate date to specific unit (day, month, etc.) |
| [date.toTimeZone](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/date/toTimeZone.html) | Convert date to timezone offset |

### [](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/#bitwise-functions-flexbitwise)
Bitwise Functions (`flex.bitwise.*`)

Low-level bitwise operations on integers.

| Function | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [bitwise.and](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/bitwise/and.html) | Bitwise AND operation |
| [bitwise.or](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/bitwise/or.html) | Bitwise OR operation |
| [bitwise.xor](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/bitwise/xor.html) | Bitwise XOR (exclusive OR) operation |
| [bitwise.not](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/bitwise/not.html) | Bitwise NOT (one’s complement) operation |
| [bitwise.shiftLeft](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/bitwise/shiftLeft.html) | Left bit shift operation |
| [bitwise.shiftRight](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/bitwise/shiftRight.html) | Right bit shift with sign extension |

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/#common-use-cases)
Common Use Cases
--------------------------------------------------------------------------

### [](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/#data-cleaning-and-normalization)
Data Cleaning and Normalization

*   `text.camelCase`, `text.snakeCase` - Normalize field names
*   `text.replace` - Remove or sanitize unwanted characters
*   `coll.union` - Deduplicate lists

### [](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/#fuzzy-matching-and-search)
Fuzzy Matching and Search

*   `text.levenshtein` - Find similar strings with edit distance
*   `text.jaroWinkler` - Match names and short strings
*   `sim.jaccard` - Compare sets and tag similarity

### [](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/#data-aggregation-and-analysis)
Data Aggregation and Analysis

*   `date.truncate` - Group by time periods
*   `coll.frequencies` - Count occurrences
*   `map.submap` - Select relevant fields

### [](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/#api-and-data-exchange)
API and Data Exchange

*   `json.toJson`, `json.fromJsonMap` - JSON serialization
*   `map.removeKeys` - Filter sensitive data
*   `text.format` - Build formatted messages

### [](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/#permission-and-flag-management)
Permission and Flag Management

*   `bitwise.and`, `bitwise.or` - Check and set permission flags
*   `bitwise.xor` - Toggle flags

* * *

Table of contents
-----------------

*   [Bitwise Functions](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/bitwise/)
    
*   [Collection Functions](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/collections/)
    
*   [Date Functions](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/date/)

*   [Map Functions](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/map/)
    
*   [Similarity Functions](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/similarity/)
    
*   [Text Functions](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/)
    

* * *

# text.join | FalkorDB Docs

[Skip to main content](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/join.html#main-content)
 Link Menu Expand (external link) Document Search Copy Copied

[](https://www.falkordb.com/)

 Search FalkorDB Docs

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/join.html#textjoin)
text.join
=========================================================================

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/join.html#description)
Description
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Joins an array of strings into a single string using a specified delimiter.

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/join.html#syntax)
Syntax
--------------------------------------------------------------------

    flex.text.join(array, delimiter)
    

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/join.html#parameters)
Parameters
----------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `array` | list | Yes | The array of strings to join |
| `delimiter` | string | Yes | The separator to insert between elements |

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/join.html#returns)
Returns
----------------------------------------------------------------------

**Type:** string

A single string with all array elements concatenated, separated by the delimiter. Returns `null` if the array is `null` or undefined.

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/join.html#examples)
Examples
------------------------------------------------------------------------

### [](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/join.html#example-1-basic-string-joining)
Example 1: Basic String Joining

    RETURN flex.text.join(['apple', 'banana', 'cherry'], ', ') AS result
    

**Output:**

    result
    ----------------------
    apple, banana, cherry
    

### [](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/join.html#example-2-building-csv-lines)
Example 2: Building CSV Lines

    MATCH (u:User)
    WITH [u.id, u.name, u.email] AS fields
    RETURN flex.text.join(fields, ',') AS csvLine
    

### [](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/join.html#example-3-creating-tags-string)
Example 3: Creating Tags String

    MATCH (p:Post)
    RETURN p.title, flex.text.join(p.tags, ' #') AS hashtags
    

**Output:**

    title           | hashtags
    ----------------|------------------
    My First Post   | tech #coding #js
    

### [](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/join.html#example-4-building-paths)
Example 4: Building Paths

    WITH ['home', 'user', 'documents', 'file.txt'] AS parts
    RETURN flex.text.join(parts, '/') AS path
    

**Output:**

    path
    --------------------------
    home/user/documents/file.txt
    

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/join.html#notes)
Notes
------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Returns `null` if input array is `null`
*   Empty strings in the array are included in the output
*   Delimiter can be any string, including empty string
*   Commonly used for CSV generation, path building, or tag formatting

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/join.html#see-also)
See Also
------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [text.format](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/text/format.html)
     - Format strings with placeholders
*   [coll.zip](https://docs.falkordb.com/udfs/flex/collections/zip.html)
     - Combine two lists

* * *

# LIMIT | FalkorDB Docs

[Skip to main content](https://docs.falkordb.com/cypher/limit.html#main-content)
 Link Menu Expand (external link) Document Search Copy Copied

[](https://www.falkordb.com/)

 Search FalkorDB Docs

[](https://docs.falkordb.com/cypher/limit.html#limit)
LIMIT
===========================================================

Although not mandatory, you can use the limit clause to limit the number of records returned by a query:

    LIMIT <max records to return>
    

If not specified, there’s no limit to the number of records returned by a query.

* * *
